Slaughterhouse IV (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 2
Ryan: All right. So, welcome back. This is going be Themeplate 7, Slaughterhouse IV. This is a
lighter version of the themeplate above that you can use if you're using email to just
drive people to a pre-launch asset, whether that's a video, workshop, webinar,
challenge, whatever it is. So, in this case, the email's job isn't to deliver the big shift and
aha itself, but to sell consumption of the asset that does. So, you're coaching the click
more than that whole belief itself. So here we're going to mimic a shorter formula, the
AIDA. So this is Attention, Interest, Desire, and Action. And, the way it's going to break
down into themeplate is we're going to grab that attention right off the bat by calling
out that sacred cow and the sour milk it produces.
We're going to tease at that aha, but we're not going to deliver it fully. Then, we're
going to build up the benefits of shifting from that sacred cow to the aha without fully
spilling the beans and ... I mean, it's weird to say spilling and sour milk in the same
container, but we just did it. So, we'll roll with it. And then we're going to end by
pointing to the video, blog, workshop, challenge, webinar, whatever it is, to get the full
scoop.
So, an example of this would be, "I wrote this in nine minutes." Or, "How long would this
take you to write?" "I'm here to take a stand for you, Ryan. You see, You've been fed
this crazy lie that copywriting is this scary, esoteric, and super hard thing that the
average 'I just want to get my funnel up and running and make money' entrepreneur
can't handle. And the sheer bull-shittery of this wouldn't drive me so crazy if it wasn't
having such a crippling effect on your business and sanity."
So, we led off, you know, the lie that copywriting is scary, esoteric and super hard, and
inaccessible to everyday, normal online entrepreneurs. And then we get into the sour
milk. You know, pushing back your launch dates over and over again as you desperately
try to recruit a member of the only profession where it's still cool to drink before noon.
Finally launching, but cringing at your conversion rates and starting to question whether
or not people even want your "thing". Searching for more quick fixes. Those fill-in-the-
blank templates that take even more time and effort to shoehorn your message into
than it would to do it yourself the right way. Or worse, investing half your launch budget
for a robot or software that supposedly can do it for you.
Sorry. Selling is still a sentient job, a.k.a. your job, as well as a fast, fun, and [inaudible
00:02:46] profitable one once you know your way around a launch email. The second
workshop of our profit and launch series just went live. So this is where we're pointing
to the asset. In it we will give you release from endless nights going to war with your
MacBook Pro and instead show you, and here we're going to be building up desire.
We're not giving away that full aha and [inaudible 00:03:13], but we're teasing it and
showing the benefits and outcomes of applying it. And if you were part of our
prelaunch, and you're like "That's not what you guys did," I'm just going to give the
disclaimer that this was a much earlier draft. That it's following that same methodology-
-that same flow that works for this type of themeplate.

Slaughterhouse IV (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 2
So we're going to sell the asset here. Things are speeding up quickly on x date. We're
going to be releasing the third and final piece of the whole launch and profit puzzle. And
this is where you really dive in deeper into selling that asset by teasing them what
they're going to be empowered to do later. The only way to get the full benefit from
that one is if you take a few minutes right now to fully steep yourself in the [inaudible
00:04:00] we have waiting for you here.
Give it a spin. Leave a comment so you have your call to action. So that's going to be
what you do with Slaughterhouse IV. So, it's typically shorter, but this one's actually
pretty long. This is a good size length, but you're going to want to follow this format.
And the reason this one tends to go a little longer is I like to really agitate with the sour
milk. I like to spill it all over the place. If you're going to drop and spill some sour milk
you might as well empty the whole container, or at least a good portion of it.
So that's usually right off the bat, when you have attention, then you're going to tease
the aha without giving it away. Build up the benefits of shifting from it and make sure
you're pointing to the asset that delivers it. So those are the two themeplates for the
prelaunch. Just as a reminder, depending on the type of launch you're using, if you're
doing a three-part video series, you'll be sending three types of these emails to point
towards it. You could even write a whole set of these. You can write extra ones to
resend to people who didn't click through the first time. So, Slaughterhouse IV and V are
versatile, but they'll work best in that prelaunch. That's what you really want to do
reshift those beliefs. Give them those aha's and insights that are more empowering and
move them towards a point where once you launch, the outcome feels inevitable. It just
generates all that momentum and really does the heavy lifting of transforming your
buyer to someone who can say "yes" once your cart opens.
So, that's all I have for you here. Take a breather. Do the work. Get to work on these
emails, and I will see you in Module 5 where we're going to talk about the launch, which
is that moment you say, "My program is now open for sale, come and get it." Oh no,
another cow reference. It's all good. I'll meet you in the next lesson.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
